steps.title: Build the total cost
steps.content: Each container costs 1.87 dollars, so $c$ containers cost $1.87c$ dollars. Each roll of tape costs 2.40 dollars, so $t$ rolls cost $2.40t$ dollars. Total spent: $1.87c + 2.40t$.

steps.title: Apply the budget
steps.content: Leo has 15 dollars, which is the maximum he can spend. The total cost must be at most 15 dollars, giving $1.87c + 2.40t \le 15$.

steps.title: Test the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $1.87c+2.40t\le15$: Correct coefficients (containers at 1.87 dollars, tape at 2.40 dollars) and the correct $\le$ direction for a spending limit. Works.
  • Choice B — $1.87c+2.40t\ge15$: Coefficients are right, but $\ge 15$ would force Leo to spend at least 15 dollars. He only has 15 dollars. Eliminate.
  • Choice C — $2.40c+1.87t\le15$: The coefficients on $c$ and $t$ are swapped, charging the tape price for containers. Eliminate.
  • Choice D — $2.40c+1.87t\ge15$: Both the coefficients and the inequality direction are wrong. Eliminate.
That matches Choice A — $1.87c+2.40t\le15$.
